channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The iPhone 16E is here. Here are 5 ways it’s different from the iPhone SE
item.description: Apple has introduced its first new product of 2025: the iPhone 16E. The new iPhone replaces the iPhone SE from Apple’s lineup—the company’s entry-level, budget iPhone.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Google Adds Gesture Searching to Google Lens Feature | Tech Updates
item.description: Google is updating its Google Lens feature with gesture searching capabilities, making it easier to find information on-screen. The update will roll out globally this week in the Chrome and Google apps on iOS.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: New iPhone 16e renews Apple’s push into budget smartphone market
item.description: Apple has faced increased competition from Google and Samsung, in the budget smartphone market and in the AI space
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Netflix is getting its first-ever English medical procedural series and a historical drama that looks like a Spanish Bridgerton
item.description: Netflix has announced its first English-language medical procedural series and a Spanish period drama that looks straight out of Bridgerton.
item.dc:creator: grace.morris@futurenet.com (Grace Morris), TechRadar

item.title: Unitree's Humanoid Robots Steal the Show at China's Spring Festival Gala
item.description: Unitree's H1 humanoid robots perform alongside human dancers in a mesmerizing rendition of the traditional Yangge folk dance, marking a significant milestone in the world of robotics and entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intro to Elixir: A fresh take on functional programming
item.description: Elixir is one of the most interesting stories in the world of programming languages right now. Built on the Erlang ecosystem, Elixir offers a fresh take on functional programming and new ways of thinking about concurrency and fault tolerance.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Experts give up hope for 157 false killer whales stranded on a beach in Australia's Tasmania state
item.description: Marine experts have given up hope of rescuing more than 150 false killer whales that stranded on a remote beach on Australia’s island state of Tasmania.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: National Science Foundation cuts 10 percent of staff
item.description: Nearly 170 employees at the National Science Foundation (NSF) who were still on probation were fired by the Trump administration on Tuesday, union officials told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Bannon: Musk 'wants to impose his freak experiments' on US
item.description: Former White House chief adviser turned conservative commentator Steve Bannon blasted billionaire Elon Musk over his efforts to reduce the size of the federal government and gain more power in the U.S. "Musk is a parasitic illegal immigrant," he said during an interview with the website UnHerd.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: The unbearable lateness of Apple Intelligence
item.description: Apple has apparently delayed what is arguably its most important Apple Intelligence feature, contextual intelligence, by at least another month. It’s the latest chapter in what history will remember as the company’s most painfully slow, yet strategically significant, introduction yet.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump and EU Commission set course for confrontation on big tech
item.description: width="2193" height="1234" sizes="(max-width: 2193px) 100vw, 2193px">With the second Trump administration, very different cultures are once again clashing in the transatlantic relationship. rawf8 – shutterstock.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Electric SUV is the ultimate lifeline for search and rescue missions
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about the Genesis GV60 MIV, an electric rescue EV with snow tracks for extreme missions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth, consumers
item.description: Since taking office, Trump has said he would place tariffs on foreign production of computer chips and semiconductors in order to return chip manufacturing to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Avoiding the cloud migration graveyard
item.description: Fictional global retailer StyleHaven embarked on its cloud migration journey, eager to modernize its systems and improve its customers’ experience. StyleHaven opted to “lift and shift,” assuming its existing infrastructure would transfer to the cloud with few issues. The result? Chaos.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple, Google app stores amid Trump ban delay
item.description: The Chinese-owned app was removed from the stores after it briefly shut down in the U.S. to abide by a law that required TikTok’s parent company ByteDance to divest from the platform or face a Jan. 19 ban.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WGN 9

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to mature your IT infrastructure forward while also mining backward for gaps
item.description: Creating a forward-thinking framework that keeps sight of what came before makes disruption and digital maturity possible, says Leidos CIO Alexandra Guenther. The post How to mature your IT infrastructure forward while also mining backward for gaps first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, PIX 11
